slice_name: Tag a client project with a label

skeleton_fidelity.note: Slice diff is additive. project_management/project_label.py is new (C3 extended, additive file). web_ingress/project_label_dispatch.py is a new additive file. wsgi.py APPENDS only _ProjectLabelAdapter, _view_set_project_label, and path('projects/<slug>/label') — skeleton F1 routes (GET /, /auth/login, /auth/callback) and S4 routes (/projects, /projects/, /projects/<slug>/edit, /projects/<slug>/delete) are byte-unchanged. No frozen skeleton component internals rewritten. No frozen skeleton or S4 oracle test re-greened or edited. Baseline extended, not reshaped (H14/B8).

issues.finding: Entire module freelancer_app/tags/tagService.py is introduced in a new top-level package 'tags' that no component in the S5 build_set (C3) or prior build_set owns. CONVENTION_BASELINE mandates one package per component (src/freelancer_app/<component_snake>/*.py); C3's namespace is project_management. TagService carries no R*/AC* trace from the S5 build_set (R11/R13 are satisfied by project_management/project_label.py + project_label_dispatch.py). No CT* obligation, no flow step, no ADR mandate references this module or the tags/ package. Code is untraceable drift — a hostile reviewer blocks demo because an unowned, untraceable module ships to production with no requirement behind it, and the CONVENTION_BASELINE package structure is violated without a recorded decision.
issues.fix_hint: Delete src/freelancer_app/tags/tagService.py and the tags/ package. Label capability is fully satisfied by project_management/project_label.py (C3, build_set file). No separate package or class should be introduced outside the declared component namespace for this feature.

issues.finding: setLabel wraps self.dataStore.update_project(...) in a bare 'except Exception: return None' that swallows CT2 extended failure modes StoreUnavailableError and ConstraintViolationError. CT2 extended failure modes (from .hld/slices/S5/contracts.json) require these errors to propagate unmodified — the baseline propagate-not-catch idiom (project_label.py, project_store.py) and test_ct2_label_failure_store_unavailable / test_ct2_label_failure_constraint_violation both assert that the caller receives the raised exception. Returning None on a store-unavailable or constraint-violation fakes success to the caller and silently loses the label write in production — a hostile reviewer blocks demo because the failure path that CT2 mandates is hidden and the running app fabricates success on real persistence failure.
issues.fix_hint: Remove the try/except entirely (or remove this file per I1 fix). CT2 failure modes must propagate unmodified; no catch-all is permitted around the store call. The correct pattern is the bare delegation in project_label.py:ProjectLabeler.set_label — no try/except.
